2605.10923.title: Dynamic Skill Lifecycle Management for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2605.10923.paper_summary: Large language model agents increasingly rely on external skills to solve complex tasks, where skills act as modular units that extend their capabilities beyond what parametric memory alone supports. Existing methods assume external skills either accumulate as persistent guidance or internalized into the policy, eventually leading to zero-skill inference. We argue this assumption is overly restrictive, since with limited parametric capacity and uneven marginal contribution across skills, the optimal active skill set is non-monotonic, task- and stage-dependent. In this work, we propose SLIM, a framework of dynamic Skill LIfecycle Management for agentic reinforcement learning (RL), which treats the active external skill set as a dynamic optimization variable jointly updated with policy learning. Specifically, SLIM estimates each active skill's marginal external contribution through leave-one-skill-out validation, then applies three lifecycle operations: retaining high-value skills, retiring skills whose contribution becomes negligible after sufficient exposure, and expanding the skill bank when persistent failures reveal missing capability coverage. Experiments show that SLIM outperforms the best baselines by an average of 7.1% points across ALFWorld and SearchQA. Results further indicate that policy learning and external skill retention are not mutually exclusive: some skills are absorbed into the policy, while others continue to provide external value, supporting SLIM as a more general paradigm for skill-based agentic RL.
2605.10923.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型代理越來越依賴外部技能來解決複雜任務，其中技能作為模組單位，擴展其能力超出僅依賴參數記憶所支持的範圍。現有方法假設外部技能要麼作為持久指導累積，要麼內化到策略中，最終導致零技能推斷。我們認為這一假設過於限制，因為在有限的參數容量和技能之間不均勻的邊際貢獻下，最佳的主動技能集是非單調的，依賴於任務和階段。在這項工作中，我們提出了SLIM，一個動態技能生命週期管理框架，用於代理強化學習（RL），它將主動外部技能集視為一個動態優化變量，與策略學習共同更新。具體來說，SLIM通過留一技能驗證來估計每個主動技能的邊際外部貢獻，然後應用三個生命週期操作：保留高價值技能、在經過充分暴露後退役貢獻變得微不足道的技能，以及在持續失敗顯示缺失能力覆蓋時擴展技能庫。實驗顯示，SLIM在ALFWorld和SearchQA上平均超過最佳基線7.1個百分點。結果進一步表明，策略學習和外部技能保留並非互斥：一些技能被吸收進策略中，而另一些則繼續提供外部價值，支持SLIM作為一種更通用的基於技能的代理強化學習範式。
2605.10923.author: Junhao Shen et.al.
2605.10923.authors: Junhao Shen, Teng Zhang, Xiaoyan Zhao, Hong Cheng
